MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-19-2015 Set For Case Management Conference On Jun-24-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alt Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-03-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS DTZ AMERICAS, INC. AND CHRISTOPHER JOHNKE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - Motion for summary judgment granted. The Court finds good cause for hearing this motion within 30 days of trial. The Declaration of Robert M. Pattison establishes that the motion was scheduled on the first available hearing date. As to causes of action 1-6, Defendants met their initial burden of showing that Plaintiff cannot establish any nexus between her race and any perceived harassment, discrimination, or retaliation. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 1-52.) Defendants have also met their initial burden by showing that there were legitimate, non-discriminatory reasons for any action taken against Plaintiff. (UMF 8-10, 12, 16-19, 35, 38.) Plaintiff has not met her burden of producing "substantial responsive evidence that the employer's showing was untrue or pretextual." (Hersant v. California Dept. of Social Services (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 997, 1004-05.) Plaintiff admits in her deposition that she did not overhear her coworkers make statements about her race. (UMF 48.) Additionally, the Declaration of Bridget Wayne is insufficient to raise an inference that there is a nexus between any alleged harassment, discrimination, or retaliation, and Plaintiff's race. Wayne's statement that she overhead Christopher Johnke state that Plaintiff is a Mexican is not substantial evidence of a nexus. (See Declaration of Bridget Wayne, par. 7.) No context for the statement is provided. Further, it is undisputed that Johnke did not play a role in Plaintiff's termination or placement on a performance improvement plan. (UMF 20-21.) As to the 8th cause of action for failure to pay wages, Defendants met their initial burden of showing that Plaintiff cannot establish that she worked overtime. (UMF 58.) Plaintiff does not dispute the fact that she worked "from around 8:30 or 9 in the morning until 5 or 6 at night" and has therefore not raised a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendants failed to pay overtime. As to the 9th cause of action for failure to provide meal and rest breaks, Defendants met their initial burden establishing that the company provides meal and rest breaks and that Plaintiff was never instructed not to take a meal or rest break. (UMF 59.) Plaintiff does not raise a triable issue of material fact. Brinker Restaurant Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 1004 holds that the employer must provide the opportunity for a lunch and rest break, but that the employer does not have to ensure that the employee takes a break. As to the 7th and 10th causes of action, these causes of action are derivative of the other wage and hour causes of action, and therefore summary adjudication as to these causes of action is granted. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 =302/ehg
